Fixed double nav bar in semester page ,
fixed add subject in command+k shortcut ,
removed the extra text appearance on navbar.

prs.title: Selection: allow selecting elements when partially covered
prs.body:
I’ve made an improvement to the selection tool: now elements can be selected as soon as the selection box touches them.
I’ve tested it locally and it works. Just checking if this is something you want in the main repo!

prs.title: created database schema and removed unnecessary files

issues.title: Request: Liquipedia (Esports Wiki)

labels.name: new icon
labels.description: Issues or pull requests for adding a new icon.

labels.name: permissions in review
labels.description: We are checking if permission is needed to include this icon. Take no action in the meantime.

issues.body:
### Brand Name

Liquipedia

### Website

https://liquipedia.net/

### Popularity Metric

Liquipedia's Global Traffic Rank (Similarweb/Semrush): #1,326 (or similar, within the last month). This rank is well within the top 500,000 qualification threshold.


### Forbidden Brands

- [x] I have reviewed the list of [forbidden brands](https://github.com/simple-icons/simple-icons/blob/develop/CONTRIBUTING.md#forbidden-brands) and can confirm the brand I am requesting is not one of them, nor is it a subsidiary of one of them.

### Terms of Service

https://liquipedia.net/hub/Liquipedia:Policy

### Official Resources for Icon and Color

SVG: (No direct link available)
Color: `#042B4C`, confirmed from official brand asset resources.

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/db9861c5-26c8-4ed1-a7bf-44bb9ff8d7aa)

### Additional Comments

Global Industry Dominance: Liquipedia is the definitive, world's largest, and most authoritative wiki for Esports (covering major titles like Dota 2, CS, VALORANT, etc.). This position of critical influence in the gaming industry strongly supports its inclusion.

Legal Note: The legal risk is low, as the icon is a well-known mark used for nominative purposes, and its policies focus on the licensing of third-party content, not on the reference use of its own logo.

issues.title: Request: Counter Strike 2

labels.name: new icon
labels.description: Issues or pull requests for adding a new icon.

labels.name: permissions in review
labels.description: We are checking if permission is needed to include this icon. Take no action in the meantime.

issues.body:
### Brand Name

Counter Strike 2

### Website

https://www.counter-strike.net/cs2

### Popularity Metric

17,011 (Statvoo — global rank for counter-strike.net). This is well inside the top 500,000 threshold.

### Forbidden Brands

- [x] I have reviewed the list of [forbidden brands](https://github.com/simple-icons/simple-icons/blob/develop/CONTRIBUTING.md#forbidden-brands) and can confirm the brand I am requesting is not one of them, nor is it a subsidiary of one of them.

### Terms of Service

Valve / Counter-Strike site terms: https://www.valvesoftware.com/legal/site-terms-of-use  (use this to check any site-specific restrictions).

### Official Resources for Icon and Color

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/85c6f454-b859-4c9e-bd4a-6636ad93e2b7)

### Additional Comments

There are a few variants of the Counter-Strike 2 mark on the web (plain black/white mark, orange backgrounds, and reversed variants). Use the primary mark for the Simple Icons entry and include the source links.

The uploaded SVG here is a clean vector (Wikimedia / official sources). I will convert it to a Simple Icons–compliant single-path, 24×24 viewBox monochrome SVG in the PR if requested (Simple Icons requires a single <path> and viewBox="0 0 24 24").

Licensing: the Wikimedia copy is hosted on Commons; check the file page for the licence details. If you prefer, use Valve’s press/brand materials as canonical source and cite that in the PR.

issues.title: Improve selection tool to require minimum coverage
issues.body:
Hey! I noticed that right now you can only select elements if the selection box fully covers them. It’d be awesome if elements could be selected as soon as the selection box touches any part of them — makes selecting small shapes or text way easier.

I’ve already written some code for this, just wanted to check if this change is something you’d like before I open a PR.
